channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Rings of Power season 2 ending explained: who dies, The Stranger's name revealed, will there be a season 3, and more big questions answered
item.description: TechRadar and The Rings of Power cast are here to provide answers to your biggest queries about season 2's final episode.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Accenture — Nvidia deal: A first peek into the new world of genAI-centric strategies
item.description: When Accenture on Wednesday rolled out a new partnership with Nvidia, including the creation of a 30,000-person Nvidia business unit, it could be seen as just another partnership expansion. And a couple of years ago, that would have been fair.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Thursday, October 3 (game #480)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Dirty romance novels are shedding their stigma thanks to TikTok
item.description: By now everyone is familiar with #BookTok—but have you stumbled across its spicier cousin #SmutTok? “Smut” refers to books that include one or more sexually explicit scenes and surprisingly (or perhaps unsurprisingly) the erotic category has found a growing fan base on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fix for BGP routing insecurity ‘plagued by software vulnerabilities’ of its own, researchers find
item.description: Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) is not yet the simple fix for the security weaknesses of the internet’s Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) many in the communications industry think it is, a team of German researchers has warned.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok Trend Turns Sour: "She Deserved the Purse" Movement Causes Concern
item.description: A viral TikTok trend encouraging people to hide gift cards in baby product aisles has taken a negative turn as users started damaging products in search of hidden cash.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Silicon Valley has a plan to save humanity: Just flip on the nuclear reactors
item.description: Electricity consumption from data centers, AI and crypto mining (its own environmental headache) could double by 2026, according to the International Energy Agency.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newsom throws AI regulation fight into uncertainty with veto
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom's (D) decision to veto a sweeping artificial intelligence (AI) bill has renewed the debate over the future of regulation of the technology, leaving different sectors of the tech industry largely in disagreement on the best path forward.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Mapping the Great Lakes: A Quest for Precision
item.description: Great Lakes mapping effort aims to pinpoint shipwrecks, topographical features, and infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency
item.description: Do you know your true cloud costs? The post Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: PyTorch library makes models faster and smaller
item.description: The PyTorch Foundation, makers of the PyTorch machine learning framework, has launched torchao, a PyTorch native library that makes models faster and smaller by leveraging low-bit dtypes, sparsity, and quantization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Appeals court reinstates Indiana lawsuit against TikTok alleging child safety, privacy concerns
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Indiana Court of Appeals has reinstated a lawsuit filed by the state accusing TikTok of deceiving its users about the video-sharing platform's level of inappropriate content for children and the security of its consumers’ personal information.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft Copilot gets Voice and Vision features
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday announced several updates to its free Microsoft Copilot and paid-for Copilot Pro services aimed at making the personal AI assistant more powerful and easier to converse with. Among the updates is Copilot Vision.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM expands Nvidia GPU options for cloud customers
item.description: IBM is offering expanded access to Nvidia GPUs on IBM Cloud to help enterprise customers advance their AI implementations, including large language model (LLM) training.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US modifies AI chip export rules, boosting Middle East access
item.description: In a move aimed at facilitating the global development and supply of artificial intelligence (AI), the US Commerce Department has brought a new policy that will ease export restrictions for advanced AI chips to data centers in the Middle East and Central Asia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to use Loop components in Microsoft 365 apps
item.description: Microsoft’s ambitious collaboration app, Microsoft Loop, includes shared workspaces as well as portable content snippets called Loop components. These components can be shared and embedded in multiple Microsoft 365 apps.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever
item.description: It’s a case of “haves and have-nots.” Here’s an informal run down of agencies with special cyber pay authorities, including DoD, DHS, and the VA. The post Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel launches Xeon 6 processors and Gaudi 3 AI accelerators
item.description: Intel has formally launched its next Xeon 6 server processors as well as the Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, making some pretty big boasts in the process.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortnite maker accuses Samsung, Google of colluding to block app store competitors
item.description: Fortnite maker Epic Games filed a lawsuit Monday accusing Google and Samsung, the world’s largest Android maker, of conspiring to block third-party competitors of the Google Play Store.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Intel’s Altera spinout launches FPGA products, software
item.description: Altera – Intel’s standalone company focused on FPGA hardware – introduced an array of FPGA hardware, software, and services at its annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arm, Intel, and the vengeance of Newton
item.description: Arm’s attempt to purchase part of Intel’s computer chip business may have been declined, but it once again demonstrates the important role played in the evolution of today’s tech by Apple and its handheld Newton.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: California Governor Newsom vetoes AI safety bill, arguing it’s ‘not the best approach’
item.description: In a significant move, California Governor Gavin Newsom vetoed a highly contested artificial intelligence (AI) safety bill on Sunday, citing concerns over its potential to stifle innovation and impose excessive restrictions on AI technologies, including basic functions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: San Francisco rolls out AI-powered cameras to combat crime
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses San Francisco deploying three advanced mobile surveillance cameras to deter crime in high-risk areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crescendo makes AI boring—and profitable
item.description: There’s money in AI, but not where you think. Sure, Nvidia and the clouds are cleaning up, but they’re primarily selling to enterprises that are mostly kicking tires, not running mission-critical AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: The decision is a blow to efforts attempting to rein in the industry that is rapidly evolving with little oversight.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Android's latest nightmare: millions of devices infected by sneaky malware
item.description: There have been cases when widely downloaded apps from the Play Store are infected with malware. Kurt the CyberGuy tackles the issue of a new Trojan malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruise ship surgery that slices giants for mega profits
item.description: A whopping 30 million people will have taken cruises in 2024, and some ships being built to keep up with this demand cost as much as $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk faces $1.9 million fine to end X ban in Brazil
item.description: Payment would allow X to “immediately return to its activities in national territory,” according to the country's court.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kobiton preps AI-enabled testing tools for mobile apps
item.description: Mobile apps tester Kobiton announced plans to roll out an AI issue aggregation engine early next year. The AI-driven engine will allow users to group related errors and identify shared commonalities across test sessions, the company said. Kobiton made the announcement September 26.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cloudflare accelerates its network with security, traffic optimizations
item.description: Cloudflare unwrapped a series of networking and security innovations during its birthday week event. The announcements include implementations of new and emerging internet standards that could have a wide-ranging impact over time.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
